text:
The Dancing Hollows earned their name. You understand why immediately: the landscape moves. Not with seismic violence, but with something worse — grace. Hills rise and fall on a rhythm you almost recognise. Rock formations extend and retract in patterns that suggest choreography.

At the hollow's centre, a structure that was once a temple revolves slowly on an invisible axis, its architecture rearranging itself with each quarter-turn. The stained glass in its windows depicts a creature that cannot decide what shape to be, cycling through them with evident pleasure.

From within the revolving temple, the sound of bells. Not warning bells. Performance bells.
lore: The Gilded Predator was worshipped as a god of motion by a civilisation that believed standing still was a form of death. They built the Dancing Hollows as an arena worthy of its performance. The civilisation is gone. The performance continues.
